source_name: EPA eGRID 2023 — US average CO2e emission rate
notes: US average location-based grid intensity from eGRID2023 data (released March 2026). National average ~0.37-0.39 kgCO2e/kWh.

source_name: Educated guess — lifecycle emissions for wind/solar electricity
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Wind ~0.01-0.02 kgCO2e/kWh and solar PV ~0.04-0.05 kgCO2e/kWh lifecycle (manufacturing + installation) per IPCC AR5 Annex III. 0.05 is conservative for mixed renewable portfolio.

source_name: EPA USEEIO v2.0 — water supply chain emissions
notes: Screening factor from USEEIO model for municipal/industrial water supply. Equivalent to ~0.35 kgCO2e/m3.

source_name: EPA USEEIO v2.0 — wastewater treatment emissions
notes: Screening factor from USEEIO model for wastewater treatment. Equivalent to ~0.70 kgCO2e/m3.

source_name: NETL CO2 Sources Report (2013) — natural gas boiler steam proxy
notes: Natural-gas-fired LP steam: ~0.07-0.12 kgCO2e/kg depending on boiler efficiency and fuel source. 0.10 is mid-range screening value.

source_name: EPA USEEIO v2.0 — alkali manufacturing supply chain
notes: Caustic soda (chlor-alkali) cradle-to-gate GWP ~0.9-1.5 kgCO2e/kg depending on allocation with Cl2 co-product. 1.10 is screening mid-value from USEEIO supply chain factors.

source_name: EPA USEEIO v2.0 — sulfuric acid manufacturing
notes: H2SO4 is produced exothermically from sulfur; low cradle-to-gate GWP ~0.08-0.15 kgCO2e/kg. Screening value from USEEIO.

source_name: EPA USEEIO v2.0 — phosphoric acid manufacturing
notes: Wet-process H3PO4 from phosphate rock: ~0.8-1.5 kgCO2e/kg including mining and processing. 1.00 is screening mid-value.

source_name: EPA USEEIO v2.0 — inorganic salt manufacturing proxy
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: MgCl2 from brine evaporation or Mg metal + HCl. USEEIO provides generic inorganic chemical factors. 0.45 is screening proxy; range 0.3-0.8 depending on production route.

source_name: NETL Cradle-to-Gate LCA Model for Alternative Sources of CO2 (2013)
notes: Post-combustion capture from fossil point source includes compression and transport. NETL 2013 report models 0.08-0.18 kgCO2e/kg depending on source and transport distance.

source_name: NETL CO2 Sources Report (2013) + GREET model — corn ethanol fermentation CO2
notes: Biogenic CO2 from corn ethanol fermentation off-gas: carbon is part of short-term cycle (net zero atmospheric). Burden is only compression + liquefaction + transport at ~0.03 kgCO2e/kg per NETL and Argonne GREET data (~110 kWh/t CO2 compression).

source_name: Fasihi et al. (2019) Techno-economic assessment of CO2 DAC plants — J Clean Prod
notes: Fasihi Efimova and Breyer 2019 J Clean Prod 224:957-980 model DAC energy penalty. High electricity and heat demand yields ~0.3-0.6 kgCO2e/kg-CO2 captured depending on energy source. 0.48 is mid-case with mixed grid. Sensitivity case only.

source_name: EPA USEEIO v2.0 — generic polymer membrane manufacturing proxy
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Polymer membrane manufacturing (PTFE/PP) cradle-to-gate. No specific membrane LCI widely available. 8.0 kgCO2e/m2 is rough proxy from USEEIO plastics manufacturing sector. Range could be 5-15.

source_name: Standard microbial biomass empirical formula CH1.8O0.5N0.2
notes: Carbon mass fraction 44-52% for bacterial biomass; 0.47 widely cited for single-cell protein. Matassa et al. 2016 Water Research reports HOB composition data. Consistent with generic formula C=12/(12+1.8+8+2.8)=0.487.

source_name: NOPA/USB (2024) Life Cycle Assessment of US Soybeans — soy meal cradle-to-gate GWP
notes: Soy meal cradle-to-gate GWP without LUC ~0.3-0.7 kgCO2e/kg (USB/NOPA 2024 LCA study). 0.50 is conservative mid-value for system expansion credit when SCP displaces soy meal protein. With LUC credit could be up to 3.3 kgCO2e/kg.
